title: Brazil vs Morocco Top Picks & Betting Odds – World Cup 2026 Preview - Yahoo Sports
original_text:
Brazil vs Morocco Top Picks & Betting Odds – World Cup 2026 Preview
Brazil vs Morocco | FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C, Matchday 3 | MetLife Stadium, East Rutherford, NJ | Saturday, June 13, 2026, 6:00 PM ET | TV: Fox, Telemundo
What's At Stake
By Matchday 3 in Group C, both Brazil and Morocco will likely know exactly what they need. Brazil enter as the group's most decorated side and the team the market expects to advance, but a slow start or an early stumble could mean this fixture carries knockout-round weight before the knockout round arrives. Morocco, fresh off their historic 2022 semi-final run and now arriving as African champions, will be targeting nothing less than a second successive deep tournament run, and a result here would send a statement to every contender left in the draw.
Verdict
Brazil vs Morocco odds make the Selecao clear favorites at -145, and the underlying evidence supports that price: Carlo Ancelotti's side carry vastly superior World Cup pedigree and an attacking roster that Morocco's remodelled defence will struggle to contain across 90 minutes. Brazil to win is the headline pick, with the case for under 2.5 goals equally compelling given Morocco's defensive identity and the tournament-stage caution both sides will exercise.
Advertisement
Best World Cup 2026 Offer Parimatch 4.8 Welcome Bonus Sports Welcome Offer Bet and Get for a £20 Free Bet BET NOW
Brazil arrive at this fixture carrying the weight of expectation that never leaves the five-time world champions, and under C. Ancelotti they have shown a more structured, pragmatic side than previous editions. Ancelotti has prioritised defensive organisation and midfield control, building around the brilliance of Vinicius Junior and Raphinha rather than asking his attackers to rescue unbalanced teams. With Neymar's return adding a veteran presence, this is a Brazil side that blends generational transition with genuine world-class quality.
Morocco, managed by H. Regragui, arrive as the side that stunned the world in Qatar and have since added the Africa Cup of Nations title. Their qualifying campaign was flawless, five wins from five, scoring 12 and conceding just once. Yet there is turbulence beneath the surface: key winger Abdessamad Ezzalzouli is expected to miss the tournament with a knee ligament injury, and the squad carries the psychological weight of a contested continental final. Youssef En-Nesyri, who scored the memorable winner against Portugal in 2022, also did not make the squad.
Advertisement
The game is likely to be won or lost in the space between Morocco's defensive block and Brazil's creative midfield. If Ancelotti's side can draw Morocco out with patient ball circulation and then exploit the width through Vinicius Junior and Raphinha, the goals will come. Morocco's best route to a result is to stay compact, transition quickly through Achraf Hakimi's overlapping runs, and hope Brahim Diaz can conjure something from limited possession.
Team Form
Brazil's Last Five Results
Croatia (N): Won 3-1 (Friendly, March 2026)
France (N): Lost 1-2 (Friendly, March 2026)
Tunisia (N): Drew 1-1 (Friendly, November 2025)
Senegal (N): Won 2-0 (Friendly, November 2025)
Japan (A): Lost 2-3 (Kirin Cup, October 2025)
Brazil's recent form is a study in inconsistency, two losses in their last five and a draw against Tunisia. However, the wins over Croatia and Senegal showed genuine quality when the squad is properly motivated, and neither of those losses came in a context that mirrors World Cup intensity. Ancelotti will take encouragement from the attacking output when his side has clicked.
Advertisement
Morocco's Last Five Results
Paraguay (N): Won 2-1 (Friendly, March 2026)
Ecuador (N): Drew 1-1 (Friendly, March 2026)
Senegal (H): Won 3-0 (Africa Cup Of Nations, January 2026)
Nigeria (H): Drew 0-0 (Africa Cup Of Nations, January 2026)
Cameroon (H): Won 2-0 (Africa Cup Of Nations, January 2026)
Morocco's form is solid without being spectacular. Three wins in five, including a commanding 3-0 over Senegal in continental competition, underlines their defensive foundation, though the goalless draw with Nigeria hints at the creative limitations they face when opponents set up to frustrate them. The loss of Ezzalzouli makes that creative problem considerably harder to solve against elite opposition.
Brazil vs Morocco Head-To-Head
These sides have met three times, and the record leans toward Brazil despite a recent shift. Brazil dominated historically, winning 3-0 in a 1998 FIFA World Cup group-stage clash and 2-0 in a 1997 friendly. Morocco reversed that trend in March 2023, claiming a 2-1 friendly win. That 2023 result is the only data point of genuine recent relevance, but a single friendly on neutral ground is a thin basis to project a World Cup upset. Brazil's World Cup pedigree in this fixture is emphatic.
Advertisement
Morocco 2-1 Brazil (Friendly, March 2023)
Brazil 3-0 Morocco (FIFA World Cup, June 1998)
Brazil 2-0 Morocco (Friendly, October 1997)
Team News
Brazil have a squad packed with Premier League and La Liga regulars, but the headline team news centres on Neymar. The 34-year-old has made the squad after a prolonged period away from international soccer, and his fitness and sharpness will be the subject of intense scrutiny. Ancelotti has options across every line, and with Marquinhos as the senior defensive presence and Alisson in goal, the backbone of the side is settled. The competition for midfield places is fierce, with Bruno Guimares, Casemiro, Lucas Paqueta and Fabinho all available.
Morocco face a more significant injury concern. Ezzalzouli's absence removes one of their most dangerous wide options, and Nayef Aguerd arrives at the tournament having not played since March due to injury, raising questions about his match sharpness in central defence. Achraf Hakimi captains the side and remains their most important player, while Sofyan Amrabat provides the combative midfield screening Morocco depend on to stay defensively organised.
Predicted Lineups
Brazil Predicted XI: Alisson; Danilo Luiz, Marquinhos (c), Gabriel Magalhaes, Alex Sandro; Casemiro, Bruno Guimares; Raphinha, Lucas Paqueta, Vinicius Junior; Endrick
Advertisement
Morocco Predicted XI:Yassine Bounou; Achraf Hakimi (c), Nayef Aguerd, Chadi Riad, Noussair Mazraoui; Sofyan Amrabat, Azzedine Ounahi; Brahim Diaz, Bilal El Khannouss, Abde Ezzalzouli; Ayoub El Kaabi
Predicted lineups based on available squad information. Squads to be confirmed by kick-off.
Key Tactical Matchup
The contest that shapes this game is Vinicius Junior against Morocco's right-side defensive structure. With Hakimi bombing forward at every opportunity, the space behind him is Morocco's greatest vulnerability, and Vinicius Junior, who has scored nine goals in 49 caps for Brazil, is precisely the type of explosive, direct attacker designed to punish that gap. Ancelotti will almost certainly instruct Vinicius Junior to attack the channel behind Hakimi rather than engage him in a conventional wide duel. If Hakimi is forced to choose between pressing high and tracking back, Morocco's defensive shape will be stretched. This is the central chess problem for H. Regragui's side to solve before kick-off.

Main Pick: Brazil to Win @ -145 (BetOnline). Brazil's attacking depth, World Cup experience across 22 appearances, and Morocco's injury disruption all point toward the Selecao. Five world titles and a squad built around Real Madrid, Arsenal and Barcelona starters makes this price justifiable even against a resilient Morocco side.
Goals Market: Under 2.5 Goals @ -125 (BetOnline). Morocco's qualifying campaign produced just 12 goals across five matches while conceding once, and their defensive identity under Regragui is built on compactness and patience. Brazil's form has been patchy enough to suggest a tight rather than a wide-open affair. The under is the sharper play on the totals line.
Scorer Market: Raphinha Anytime Scorer. Raphinha is Brazil's most in-form attacking threat in recent games, and his set-piece delivery and direct running make him a consistent goal threat. His output in the recent qualifying and friendly cycle places him among Brazil's most productive attackers heading into this tournament.
Brazil vs Morocco Best Bets Combination: Brazil to Win and Under 2.5 Goals. A controlled, narrow Brazil victory is the most logical outcome given Morocco's defensive structure and Brazil's attacking quality. A 1-0 correct score pick is an extension of this read for those who want the longer price.
Brazil vs Morocco Betting Odds Across Operators
Here is a snapshot of the best available Brazil vs Morocco betting odds across the three approved operators:
BetOnline offers the best price on Brazil to win at -145. BetNow leads on the draw at +290, and both BetOnline and BetNow share the top Morocco win price at +450.
How To Watch and How To Bet
How To Watch
Brazil vs Morocco is broadcast in the United States on Fox and Telemundo. Kick-off is at 6:00 PM ET on June 13, 2026, at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey. International viewers can find coverage on Globo and SporTV (Brazil), beIN Sports and TF1 (France), ITV and BBC (UK), and TSN and RDS (Canada).
How To Bet
New to betting on the FIFA World Cup 2026? Here is a straightforward eight-step process:

Responsible Gambling
Gambling should be treated as entertainment and approached with clear limits on time and money spent. If you or someone you know is struggling with problem gambling, contact the National Council on Problem Gambling helpline at 1-800-GAMBLER (1-800-426-2537), available 24 hours a day, seven days a week. Additional support is available through Gamblers Anonymous. Always gamble responsibly.
